articles.title: Maharashtra weather news: IMD issues heatwave alert in multiple regions including Mumbai, Vidarbha, Marathwada, Konkan and...., rainfall to begin across North India from...; Check full IMD forecast - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
Maharashtra weather news: IMD issues heatwave alert in multiple regions including Mumbai, Vidarbha, Marathwada, Konkan and…., rainfall to begin across North India from…; Check full IMD forecast
According to the IMD's latest Monsoon Update 2026, rainfall is expected to begin across parts of North India from June 18, while heatwave conditions are likely to persist in Vidarbha, Madhya Maharashtra, and Marathwada until the specified dates.
According to the IMD's latest Monsoon Update 2026, rainfall is expected to begin across parts of North India from June 18, while heatwave conditions are likely to persist in Vidarbha, Madhya Maharashtra, and Marathwada until the specified dates.
Heatwave Conditions Continue In Parts Of Maharashtra
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a fresh weather update, saying that while a new Western Disturbance is expected to bring relief to northwestern India, some parts of Maharashtra will continue to experience intense heat. Vidarbha is likely to remain under heatwave conditions until June 19. Meanwhile, parts of Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada may face severe heat on June 18 and 19.
Hot And Humid Weather In Konkan And Goa
Residents of Konkan and Goa are expected to experience hot and humid weather between June 16 and June 19. According to the IMD, maximum temperatures across Maharashtra could rise by around 2°C over the next few days before dropping by 2-3°C later in the week.
Monsoon Advances Towards More States
The southwest monsoon is currently advancing through several regions, including Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadrachalam, Koraput, Ranchi and Muzaffarpur. Weather conditions are expected to remain favourable over the next four to five days for the monsoon to move further into the remaining parts of Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and Chhattisgarh.
Rain Alert For Northwestern India
A fresh Western Disturbance is expected to bring widespread rainfall to northwestern India between June 18 and June 22. Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand are likely to receive light to moderate rainfall during this period. Hailstorms are also possible in parts of Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand on June 16, 18 and 19.
Heavy Rainfall Likely In Northeast India
Most parts of Northeast India, including Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura, are expected to receive rainfall throughout the week from June 16 to June 22. Some areas could witness heavy to extremely heavy rainfall. Similar conditions are expected in Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim between June 17 and June 22. The IMD also noted that Banda in Uttar Pradesh recorded the country's highest maximum temperature at 43.4°C during the past 24 hours. In Maharashtra, strong winds with speeds of 40-60 kmph were reported in parts of Nashik, Nandurbar and Satara. Meanwhile, farmers across Maharashtra continue to wait for the arrival of the monsoon, which has yet to make significant progress in the state.
